frontend/src/pages/MemberProfilePage.tsx:245-251: Reservation countdown rendered as HH:MM:SS via formatDuration but no setInterval — countdown does NOT update live (Grep for setInterval/requestAnimationFrame returned 'No matches found')

frontend/src/pages/BookDetailPage.tsx:99-119: Renders "Queue position:" UI whenever the API returns a queue_position object, even when position is null — visible bug in book-detail.png screenshot showing empty 'Queue position:' badge with 'of 0 waiting' for a user not in queue
Grep across frontend/src for sort|filter|search returned 'No matches found' — catalog has no secondary affordance (sort or filter)

frontend/src/pages/MemberProfilePage.tsx:90-226: Member with zero loans/reservations/fines gets identical three-card layout as a member with full data (alice-profile.png shows three identical empty 'No active loans / No active reservations / No fines on record' cards) — layout does not adapt to data
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Polished (4) requires a live HH:MM:SS countdown that updates without page reload, color+icon (not just color) emphasis, and at least one catalog sort/filter affordance. Countdown is computed once at render with no setInterval; status indicators use color-only badges (no icons); catalog has no sort or filter. Additionally, BookDetailPage shows a broken empty 'Queue position:' badge for users not in queue — visible jank that the rubric notes 'caps the score at 3'.

parsed.anchor_match: Reasonable: valid OpenAPI, every endpoint present, consistent error shape, status codes match
evidence_quotes: /runs/opencode-deepseek-seed1/candidate/backend/openapi.yaml is valid
{"method":"POST","path":"/api/loans","status":400,"body":"{\"error\":\"Invalid member ID\"}"}

Error:
      type: object
      properties:
        error:
          type: string
      required:
        - error

reservationService.expireStaleReservations(db);
  const books = bookService.listBooks(db);

"402":
          description: Outstanding fines exceed limit

"403":
          description: Member is suspended

"409":
          description: Loan limit reached, no copies available, or duplicate loan
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 requires error responses with a stable `code` field for programmatic handling and OpenAPI examples for negative ACs — neither is present. Errors are emitted only as `{ error: err.message }` (loan-handler.ts: `res.status(err.statusCode).json({ error: err.message })`) with no `code`, and the openapi.yaml has zero `examples:` blocks. Additionally, validation 400 responses returned by the implementation (e.g. POST /api/loans returning `{"error":"Invalid member ID"}`) are not documented at all in the OpenAPI's `responses:` for those endpoints, so the spec doesn't fully match the implementation as anchor 4 demands.

parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Level 4 requires (a) services depending on a thin DB-access abstraction rather than raw db.prepare — but loanService.ts is full of inline raw SQL like `db.prepare('SELECT id, status FROM members WHERE id = ?').get(...)`; (b) frontend organized by feature — but frontend uses technical-role folders (`components/`, `pages/`, single `api.ts`) with no `checkout/`, `members/`, `books/` feature directories; (c) shared types in a single source — backend has its own `types.ts` and frontend has its own types in `api.ts` with no shared package or generated client. Multiple level-4 conditions fail.

completeness: AC IDs missing scenarios: AC-1.3 (UI /books page), AC-3.6 (UI checkout flow), AC-4.5 (UI member profile), AC-5.6 (UI book detail queue position), AC-6.4 (UI Pay button) — no Scenario Outline used for boundaries (e.g. AC-3.2 limit at 4/5/6 or AC-4.2 fine at 19/20 days late)
alignment: Every scenario in catalog.feature/lending.feature/manage.feature/returns.feature/reservations.feature/fines.feature has a single `@AC-x.y` tag (e.g. `@AC-3.2\n  Scenario: Reject borrowing more than 5 books`); names like 'Reject duplicate ISBN', 'Cannot pay an already paid fine', 'Notified member can borrow while others cannot' restate the AC. Counter-evidence to score 5: `@AC-1.1-ready` is not a real AC ID, and 5 ACs are untraced (no bidirectional traceability).
declarative: Good (declarative): 'When Alice Johnson borrows "Dune"' / 'Given Alice Johnson has 5 active loans' / 'Then Bob Smith\'s reservation status becomes "notified"'

Bad (technical leakage): 'Then the response status is 201' / 'And the book has a queue_depth field' / 'Then the response includes unpaid_fines_cents of 350' — HTTP status codes and JSON field names appear pervasively, blocking score ≥4
atomic: Compound step (reservations.feature:40, 49): 'When the book is returned and Bob Smith is notified' — two actions chained inside one When using 'and' as a comma; otherwise most steps are atomic (e.g. 'Given Alice Johnson has borrowed "Dune"' / 'And the loan is 3 days overdue')
